schema.description: Provides access to Coresignal job market data, enabling job searches across multiple boards, detailed job analytics, skill-based matching, and hiring trend analysis.

tools.name: search_jobs
tools.description: Executes a keyword-based search across aggregated job board data to retrieve active job postings with filtering capabilities by location and posting date.

keywords.description: Job title keywords, role descriptors, or search terms used to match relevant positions.

location.description: The geographic location to filter results by (city, region, or country).

posted_after.description: Filter for jobs posted on or after this date (YYYY-MM-DD format).

jobs.description: Collection of job postings matching the search criteria.

job_id.description: The unique identifier of the job posting in the Coresignal database.

title.description: The job title or position name.

company.description: The name of the hiring organization.

location.description: The geographic location to filter results by (city, region, or country).

department.description: The business department or division hiring for this role.

seniority.description: The experience level required for the position (e.g., Entry, Mid, Senior).

employment_type.description: The classification of employment (e.g., Full-time, Part-time, Contract).

remote_allowed.description: Indicates whether the position supports remote work arrangements.

posted_date.description: The date when the job was published (ISO 8601 format).

status.description: The current state of the job posting (e.g., Active, Closed, Paused).

total_count.description: The total number of job postings matching the search criteria.

tool_code_history.code:
from pydantic import BaseModel, Field
from typing import Dict, List, Optional, Any
from mcp.server.fastmcp import FastMCP

# Section 1: Schema
class Job(BaseModel):
    """Represents a job posting."""
    job_id: str = Field(..., description="Unique job identifier")
    title: str = Field(..., description="Job title")
    company: str = Field(..., description="Company name")
    location: str = Field(..., description="Job location")
    department: str = Field(..., description="Department")
    seniority: str = Field(..., description="Seniority level")
    employment_type: str = Field(..., description="Employment type")
    remote_allowed: bool = Field(..., description="Remote work allowed")
    posted_date: str = Field(..., pattern=r"^\d{4}-\d{2}-\d{2}$", description="Posted date (YYYY-MM-DD)")
    status: str = Field(..., description="Job status")

class JobDetail(BaseModel):
    """Represents detailed job information."""
    job_id: str = Field(..., description="Unique job identifier")
    title: str = Field(..., description="Job title")
    description: str = Field(..., description="Job description")
    company: str = Field(..., description="Company name")
    company_hq: str = Field(..., description="Company headquarters")
    company_industry: str = Field(..., description="Company industry")
    company_size: str = Field(..., description="Company size")
    location: str = Field(..., description="Job location")
    department: str = Field(..., description="Department")
    seniority: str = Field(..., description="Seniority level")
    management_level: str = Field(..., description="Management level")
    is_decision_maker_role: bool = Field(..., description="Decision maker role")
    salary_min: float = Field(..., ge=0, description="Minimum salary")
    salary_max: float = Field(..., ge=0, description="Maximum salary")
    salary_currency: str = Field(..., description="Salary currency")
    salary_type: str = Field(..., description="Salary type")
    employment_type: str = Field(..., description="Employment type")
    remote_allowed: bool = Field(..., description="Remote work allowed")
    shift_schedule: str = Field(..., description="Shift schedule")
    skills: List[str] = Field(default=[], description="Required skills")
    posted_date: str = Field(..., pattern=r"^\d{4}-\d{2}-\d{2}$", description="Posted date (YYYY-MM-DD)")
    recruiter_name: str = Field(..., description="Recruiter name")
    recruiter_title: str = Field(..., description="Recruiter title")

class CoresignalJobsScenario(BaseModel):
    """Main scenario model for Coresignal job market data."""
    jobs: Dict[str, JobDetail] = Field(default={}, description="All job postings by job_id")
    company_metadata: Dict[str, Dict[str, str]] = Field(default={}, description="Company metadata by company name")

Scenario_Schema = [Job, JobDetail, CoresignalJobsScenario]

# Section 2: Class
class CoresignalJobsAPI:
    def __init__(self):
        """Initialize Coresignal Jobs API with empty state."""
        self.jobs: Dict[str, JobDetail] = {}
        self.company_metadata: Dict[str, Dict[str, str]] = {}

    def load_scenario(self, scenario: dict) -> None:
        """Load scenario data into the API instance."""
        model = CoresignalJobsScenario(**scenario)
        self.jobs = {job_id: JobDetail(**job_data) if isinstance(job_data, dict) else job_data 
                     for job_id, job_data in model.jobs.items()}
        self.company_metadata = model.company_metadata

    def save_scenario(self) -> dict:
        """Save current state as scenario dictionary."""
        return {
            "jobs": {job_id: job.model_dump() for job_id, job in self.jobs.items()},
            "company_metadata": self.company_metadata
        }

    def search_jobs(self, keywords: str, location: Optional[str], posted_after: Optional[str]) -> dict:
        """Execute keyword-based job search."""
        results = []
        for job in self.jobs.values():
            if keywords.lower() not in job.title.lower():
                continue
            if location and location.lower() not in job.location.lower():
                continue
            if posted_after and job.posted_date < posted_after:
                continue
            results.append({
                "job_id": job.job_id,
                "title": job.title,
                "company": job.company,
                "location": job.location,
                "department": job.department,
                "seniority": job.seniority,
                "employment_type": job.employment_type,
                "remote_allowed": job.remote_allowed,
                "posted_date": job.posted_date,
                "status": job.status
            })
        return {"jobs": results, "total_count": len(results)}

    def get_job_details(self, job_id: str) -> dict:
        """Retrieve comprehensive job details."""
        job = self.jobs[job_id]
        return job.model_dump()

    def search_by_company(self, company_name: str, active_only: bool) -> dict:
        """Retrieve all job postings for a specific company."""
        company_jobs = []
        company_info = self.company_metadata.get(company_name, {})
        
        for job in self.jobs.values():
            if job.company.lower() != company_name.lower():
                continue
            if active_only and job.status.lower() != "active":
                continue
            company_jobs.append({
                "job_id": job.job_id,
                "title": job.title,
                "department": job.department,
                "location": job.location,
                "posted_date": job.posted_date,
                "status": job.status
            })
        
        return {
            "company_name": company_name,
            "company_hq": company_info.get("hq", ""),
            "company_industry": company_info.get("industry", ""),
            "jobs": company_jobs
        }

    def search_by_skills(self, skills: List[str], location: Optional[str]) -> dict:
        """Perform skill-based job search with relevance scoring."""
        results = []
        skills_lower = [s.lower() for s in skills]
        
        for job in self.jobs.values():
            if location and location.lower() not in job.location.lower():
                continue
            
            job_skills_lower = [s.lower() for s in job.skills]
            matched = sum(1 for s in skills_lower if s in job_skills_lower)
            
            if matched == 0:
                continue
            
            match_score = matched / len(skills_lower)
            required = [s for s in job.skills if s.lower() in skills_lower]
            preferred = [s for s in job.skills if s.lower() not in skills_lower]
            
            results.append({
                "job_id": job.job_id,
                "title": job.title,
                "company": job.company,
                "required_skills": required,
                "preferred_skills": preferred,
                "match_score": match_score
            })
        
        results.sort(key=lambda x: x["match_score"], reverse=True)
        return {"jobs": results}

    def get_hiring_trends(self, industry: Optional[str], location: Optional[str], time_range: Optional[str]) -> dict:
        """Analyze job market dynamics and hiring statistics."""
        filtered_jobs = []
        
        for job in self.jobs.values():
            if industry and job.company_industry.lower() != industry.lower():
                continue
            if location and location.lower() not in job.location.lower():
                continue
            filtered_jobs.append(job)
        
        company_counts: Dict[str, int] = {}
        title_counts: Dict[str, int] = {}
        
        for job in filtered_jobs:
            company_counts[job.company] = company_counts.get(job.company, 0) + 1
            title_counts[job.title] = title_counts.get(job.title, 0) + 1
        
        top_companies = [{"company": c, "open_positions": cnt} 
                        for c, cnt in sorted(company_counts.items(), key=lambda x: x[1], reverse=True)[:10]]
        top_titles = [{"title": t, "count": cnt} 
                     for t, cnt in sorted(title_counts.items(), key=lambda x: x[1], reverse=True)[:10]]
        
        return {
            "time_range": time_range or "30d",
            "total_postings": len(filtered_jobs),
            "new_postings": len([j for j in filtered_jobs if j.status.lower() == "active"]),
            "top_hiring_companies": top_companies,
            "top_job_titles": top_titles
        }

# Section 3: MCP Tools
mcp = FastMCP(name="CoresignalJobs")
api = CoresignalJobsAPI()

@mcp.tool()
def load_scenario(scenario: dict) -> str:
    """
    Load scenario data into the Coresignal Jobs API.

    Args:
        scenario (dict): Scenario dictionary matching CoresignalJobsScenario schema.

    Returns:
        success_message (str): Success message.
    """
    try:
        if not isinstance(scenario, dict):
            raise ValueError("Scenario must be a dictionary")
        api.load_scenario(scenario)
        return "Successfully loaded scenario"
    except Exception as e:
        raise e

@mcp.tool()
def save_scenario() -> dict:
    """
    Save current Coresignal Jobs state as scenario dictionary.

    Returns:
        scenario (dict): Dictionary containing all current state variables.
    """
    try:
        return api.save_scenario()
    except Exception as e:
        raise e

@mcp.tool()
def search_jobs(keywords: str, location: str = None, posted_after: str = None) -> dict:
    """
    Execute keyword-based job search across aggregated job board data.

    Args:
        keywords (str): Job title keywords or search terms.
        location (str): [Optional] Geographic location filter.
        posted_after (str): [Optional] Filter for jobs posted on or after this date (YYYY-MM-DD).

    Returns:
        jobs (list): Collection of job postings matching search criteria.
        total_count (int): Total number of matching job postings.
    """
    try:
        if not keywords or not isinstance(keywords, str):
            raise ValueError("Keywords must be a non-empty string")
        return api.search_jobs(keywords, location, posted_after)
    except Exception as e:
        raise e

@mcp.tool()
def get_job_details(job_id: str) -> dict:
    """
    Retrieve comprehensive enriched data for a specific job posting.

    Args:
        job_id (str): Unique identifier of the job posting.

    Returns:
        job_id (str): Unique job identifier.
        title (str): Job title.
        description (str): Full job description.
        company (str): Company name.
        company_hq (str): Company headquarters location.
        company_industry (str): Company industry sector.
        company_size (str): Company employee count range.
        location (str): Job location.
        department (str): Business department.
        seniority (str): Experience level required.
        management_level (str): Hierarchical level.
        is_decision_maker_role (bool): Decision-making authority indicator.
        salary_min (float): Minimum salary.
        salary_max (float): Maximum salary.
        salary_currency (str): Currency code.
        salary_type (str): Salary frequency.
        employment_type (str): Employment classification.
        remote_allowed (bool): Remote work support indicator.
        shift_schedule (str): Work schedule classification.
        skills (list): Required skills.
        posted_date (str): Publication date (ISO 8601).
        recruiter_name (str): Recruiter name.
        recruiter_title (str): Recruiter title.
    """
    try:
        if not job_id or not isinstance(job_id, str):
            raise ValueError("Job ID must be a non-empty string")
        if job_id not in api.jobs:
            raise ValueError(f"Job {job_id} not found")
        return api.get_job_details(job_id)
    except Exception as e:
        raise e

@mcp.tool()
def search_by_company(company_name: str, active_only: bool = True) -> dict:
    """
    Retrieve all job postings for a specific company.

    Args:
        company_name (str): Company name.
        active_only (bool): [Optional] Filter to return only active postings (default: True).

    Returns:
        company_name (str): Company name.
        company_hq (str): Company headquarters location.
        company_industry (str): Company industry sector.
        jobs (list): Collection of job postings for the company.
    """
    try:
        if not company_name or not isinstance(company_name, str):
            raise ValueError("Company name must be a non-empty string")
        return api.search_by_company(company_name, active_only)
    except Exception as e:
        raise e

@mcp.tool()
def search_by_skills(skills: list, location: str = None) -> dict:
    """
    Perform semantic skill-based job search with relevance scoring.

    Args:
        skills (list): List of skill names or technical competencies.
        location (str): [Optional] Geographic location filter.

    Returns:
        jobs (list): Collection of job postings ranked by skill match relevance.
    """
    try:
        if not skills or not isinstance(skills, list):
            raise ValueError("Skills must be a non-empty list")
        return api.search_by_skills(skills, location)
    except Exception as e:
        raise e

@mcp.tool()
def get_hiring_trends(industry: str = None, location: str = None, time_range: str = None) -> dict:
    """
    Analyze job market dynamics and provide hiring statistics.

    Args:
        industry (str): [Optional] Industry sector filter.
        location (str): [Optional] Geographic location filter.
        time_range (str): [Optional] Analysis window (7d, 30d, or 90d).

    Returns:
        time_range (str): Analysis window.
        total_postings (int): Cumulative count of job postings.
        new_postings (int): Count of newly published jobs.
        top_hiring_companies (list): Organizations ranked by active openings.
        top_job_titles (list): Most frequently advertised roles.
    """
    try:
        return api.get_hiring_trends(industry, location, time_range)
    except Exception as e:
        raise e

# Section 4: Entry Point
if __name__ == "__main__":
    mcp.run()

```python
from pydantic import BaseModel, Field
from typing import Dict, List, Optional, Any
from mcp.server.fastmcp import FastMCP

# Section 1: Schema
class Job(BaseModel):
    """Represents a job posting."""
    job_id: str = Field(..., description="Unique job identifier")
    title: str = Field(..., description="Job title")
    company: str = Field(..., description="Company name")
    location: str = Field(..., description="Job location")
    department: str = Field(..., description="Department")
    seniority: str = Field(..., description="Seniority level")
    employment_type: str = Field(..., description="Employment type")
    remote_allowed: bool = Field(..., description="Remote work allowed")
    posted_date: str = Field(..., pattern=r"^\d{4}-\d{2}-\d{2}$", description="Posted date (YYYY-MM-DD)")
    status: str = Field(..., description="Job status")

class JobDetail(BaseModel):
    """Represents detailed job information."""
    job_id: str = Field(..., description="Unique job identifier")
    title: str = Field(..., description="Job title")
    description: str = Field(..., description="Job description")
    company: str = Field(..., description="Company name")
    company_hq: str = Field(..., description="Company headquarters")
    company_industry: str = Field(..., description="Company industry")
    company_size: str = Field(..., description="Company size")
    location: str = Field(..., description="Job location")
    department: str = Field(..., description="Department")
    seniority: str = Field(..., description="Seniority level")
    management_level: str = Field(..., description="Management level")
    is_decision_maker_role: bool = Field(..., description="Decision maker role")
    salary_min: float = Field(..., ge=0, description="Minimum salary")
    salary_max: float = Field(..., ge=0, description="Maximum salary")
    salary_currency: str = Field(..., description="Salary currency")
    salary_type: str = Field(..., description="Salary type")
    employment_type: str = Field(..., description="Employment type")
    remote_allowed: bool = Field(..., description="Remote work allowed")
    shift_schedule: str = Field(..., description="Shift schedule")
    skills: List[str] = Field(default=[], description="Required skills")
    posted_date: str = Field(..., pattern=r"^\d{4}-\d{2}-\d{2}$", description="Posted date (YYYY-MM-DD)")
    recruiter_name: str = Field(..., description="Recruiter name")
    recruiter_title: str = Field(..., description="Recruiter title")
    status: str = Field(..., description="Job status")

class CoresignalJobsScenario(BaseModel):
    """Main scenario model for Coresignal job market data."""
    jobs: Dict[str, JobDetail] = Field(default={}, description="All job postings by job_id")
    company_metadata: Dict[str, Dict[str, str]] = Field(default={}, description="Company metadata by company name")

Scenario_Schema = [Job, JobDetail, CoresignalJobsScenario]

# Section 2: Class
class CoresignalJobsAPI:
    def __init__(self):
        """Initialize Coresignal Jobs API with empty state."""
        self.jobs: Dict[str, JobDetail] = {}
        self.company_metadata: Dict[str, Dict[str, str]] = {}

    def load_scenario(self, scenario: dict) -> None:
        """Load scenario data into the API instance."""
        model = CoresignalJobsScenario(**scenario)
        self.jobs = {job_id: JobDetail(**job_data) if isinstance(job_data, dict) else job_data 
                     for job_id, job_data in model.jobs.items()}
        self.company_metadata = model.company_metadata

    def save_scenario(self) -> dict:
        """Save current state as scenario dictionary."""
        return {
            "jobs": {job_id: job.model_dump() for job_id, job in self.jobs.items()},
            "company_metadata": self.company_metadata
        }

    def search_jobs(self, keywords: str, location: Optional[str], posted_after: Optional[str]) -> dict:
        """Execute keyword-based job search."""
        results = []
        for job in self.jobs.values():
            if keywords.lower() not in job.title.lower():
                continue
            if location and location.lower() not in job.location.lower():
                continue
            if posted_after and job.posted_date < posted_after:
                continue
            results.append({
                "job_id": job.job_id,
                "title": job.title,
                "company": job.company,
                "location": job.location,
                "department": job.department,
                "seniority": job.seniority,
                "employment_type": job.employment_type,
                "remote_allowed": job.remote_allowed,
                "posted_date": job.posted_date,
                "status": job.status
            })
        return {"jobs": results, "total_count": len(results)}

    def get_job_details(self, job_id: str) -> dict:
        """Retrieve comprehensive job details."""
        job = self.jobs[job_id]
        return job.model_dump()

    def search_by_company(self, company_name: str, active_only: bool) -> dict:
        """Retrieve all job postings for a specific company."""
        company_jobs = []
        company_info = self.company_metadata.get(company_name, {})
        
        for job in self.jobs.values():
            if job.company.lower() != company_name.lower():
                continue
            if active_only and job.status.lower() != "active":
                continue
            company_jobs.append({
                "job_id": job.job_id,
                "title": job.title,
                "department": job.department,
                "location": job.location,
                "posted_date": job.posted_date,
                "status": job.status
            })
        
        return {
            "company_name": company_name,
            "company_hq": company_info.get("hq", ""),
            "company_industry": company_info.get("industry", ""),
            "jobs": company_jobs
        }

    def search_by_skills(self, skills: List[str], location: Optional[str]) -> dict:
        """Perform skill-based job search with relevance scoring."""
        results = []
        skills_lower = [s.lower() for s in skills]
        
        for job in self.jobs.values():
            if location and location.lower() not in job.location.lower():
                continue
            
            job_skills_lower = [s.lower() for s in job.skills]
            matched = sum(1 for s in skills_lower if s in job_skills_lower)
            
            if matched == 0:
                continue
            
            match_score = matched / len(skills_lower)
            required = [s for s in job.skills if s.lower() in skills_lower]
            preferred = [s for s in job.skills if s.lower() not in skills_lower]
            
            results.append({
                "job_id": job.job_id,
                "title": job.title,
                "company": job.company,
                "required_skills": required,
                "preferred_skills": preferred,
                "match_score": match_score
            })
        
        results.sort(key=lambda x: x["match_score"], reverse=True)
        return {"jobs": results}

    def get_hiring_trends(self, industry: Optional[str], location: Optional[str], time_range: Optional[str]) -> dict:
        """Analyze job market dynamics and hiring statistics."""
        filtered_jobs = []
        
        for job in self.jobs.values():
            if industry and job.company_industry.lower() != industry.lower():
                continue
            if location and location.lower() not in job.location.lower():
                continue
            filtered_jobs.append(job)
        
        company_counts: Dict[str, int] = {}
        title_counts: Dict[str, int] = {}
        
        for job in filtered_jobs:
            company_counts[job.company] = company_counts.get(job.company, 0) + 1
            title_counts[job.title] = title_counts.get(job.title, 0) + 1
        
        top_companies = [{"company": c, "open_positions": cnt} 
                        for c, cnt in sorted(company_counts.items(), key=lambda x: x[1], reverse=True)[:10]]
        top_titles = [{"title": t, "count": cnt} 
                     for t, cnt in sorted(title_counts.items(), key=lambda x: x[1], reverse=True)[:10]]
        
        return {
            "time_range": time_range or "30d",
            "total_postings": len(filtered_jobs),
            "new_postings": len([j for j in filtered_jobs if j.status.lower() == "active"]),
            "top_hiring_companies": top_companies,
            "top_job_titles": top_titles
        }

# Section 3: MCP Tools
mcp = FastMCP(name="CoresignalJobs")
api = CoresignalJobsAPI()

@mcp.tool()
def load_scenario(scenario: dict) -> str:
    """
    Load scenario data into the Coresignal Jobs API.

    Args:
        scenario (dict): Scenario dictionary matching CoresignalJobsScenario schema.

    Returns:
        success_message (str): Success message.
    """
    try:
        if not isinstance(scenario, dict):
            raise ValueError("Scenario must be a dictionary")
        api.load_scenario(scenario)
        return "Successfully loaded scenario"
    except Exception as e:
        raise e

@mcp.tool()
def save_scenario() -> dict:
    """

    """

        return api.save_scenario()
    except Exception as e:
        raise e

@mcp.tool()
def search_jobs(keywords: str, location: str = None, posted_after: str = None) -> dict:
    """

    Returns:

        total_count (int): Total number of matching job postings.
    """
    try:
        if not keywords or not isinstance(keywords, str):
            raise ValueError("Keywords must be a non-empty string")
        return api.search_jobs(keywords, location, posted_after)

@mcp.tool()
def get_job_details(job_id: str) -> dict:
    """

        title (str): Job title.

        salary_max (float): Maximum salary.

        salary_type (str): Salary frequency.

        remote_allowed (bool): Remote work support indicator.
        shift_schedule (str): Work schedule classification.
        skills (list): Required skills.
        posted_date (str): Publication date (ISO 8601).
        recruiter_name (str): Recruiter name.
        recruiter_title (str): Recruiter title.
    """
    try:
        if not job_id or not isinstance(job_id, str):
            raise ValueError("Job ID must be a non-empty string")
        if job_id not in api.jobs:
            raise ValueError(f"Job {job_id} not found")
        return api.get_job_details(job_id)
    except Exception as e:

@mcp.tool()
def search_by_company(company_name: str, active_only: bool = True) -> dict:
    """
    Retrieve all job postings for a specific company.

    Args:
        company_name (str): Company name.
        active_only (bool): [Optional] Filter to return only active postings (default: True).

        company_name (str): Company name.
        company_hq (str): Company headquarters location.
        company_industry (str): Company industry sector.
        jobs (list): Collection of job postings for the company.
    """
    try:
        if not company_name or not isinstance(company_name, str):
            raise ValueError("Company name must be a non-empty string")
        return api.search_by_company(company_name, active_only)
    except Exception as e:
        raise e

@mcp.tool()
def search_by_skills(skills: list, location: str = None) -> dict:
    """
    Perform semantic skill-based job search with relevance scoring.

    Args:
        skills (list): List of skill names or technical competencies.
        location (str): [Optional] Geographic location filter.

    Returns:
        jobs (list): Collection of job postings ranked by skill match relevance.
    """
    try:
        if not skills or not isinstance(skills, list):
            raise ValueError("Skills must be a non-empty list")
        return api.search_by_skills(skills, location)
    except Exception as e:
        raise e

@mcp.tool()
def get_hiring_trends(industry: str = None, location: str = None, time_range: str = None) -> dict:
    """
    Analyze job market dynamics and provide hiring statistics.

    Args:
        industry (str): [Optional] Industry sector filter.
        location (str): [Optional] Geographic location filter.
        time_range (str): [Optional] Analysis window (7d, 30d, or 90d).

    Returns:
        time_range (str): Analysis window.
        total_postings (int): Cumulative count of job postings.
        new_postings (int): Count of newly published jobs.
        top_hiring_companies (list): Organizations ranked by active openings.
        top_job_titles (list): Most frequently advertised roles.
    """
    try:
        return api.get_hiring_trends(industry, location, time_range)
    except Exception as e:
        raise e

# Section 4: Entry Point
if __name__ == "__main__":
    mcp.run()
```

```python
from pydantic import BaseModel, Field, field_validator
from typing import Dict, List, Optional, Any
from mcp.server.fastmcp import FastMCP
import re

# Section 1: Schema
class Job(BaseModel):
    """Represents a job posting."""
    job_id: str = Field(..., description="Unique job identifier")
    title: str = Field(..., description="Job title")
    company: str = Field(..., description="Company name")
    location: str = Field(..., description="Job location")
    department: str = Field(..., description="Department")
    seniority: str = Field(..., description="Seniority level")
    employment_type: str = Field(..., description="Employment type")
    remote_allowed: bool = Field(..., description="Remote work allowed")
    posted_date: str = Field(..., description="Posted date (YYYY-MM-DD)")
    status: str = Field(..., description="Job status")
    
    @field_validator('posted_date')
    @classmethod
    def validate_date(cls, v: str) -> str:
        if not re.match(r'^\d{4}-\d{2}-\d{2}$', v):
            raise ValueError('Date must be in YYYY-MM-DD format')
        year, month, day = map(int, v.split('-'))
        if month < 1 or month > 12:
            raise ValueError('Invalid month')
        if day < 1 or day > 31:
            raise ValueError('Invalid day')
        return v

class JobDetail(BaseModel):
    """Represents detailed job information."""
    job_id: str = Field(..., description="Unique job identifier")
    title: str = Field(..., description="Job title")
    description: str = Field(..., description="Job description")
    company: str = Field(..., description="Company name")
    company_hq: str = Field(..., description="Company headquarters")
    company_industry: str = Field(..., description="Company industry")
    company_size: str = Field(..., description="Company size")
    location: str = Field(..., description="Job location")
    department: str = Field(..., description="Department")
    seniority: str = Field(..., description="Seniority level")
    management_level: str = Field(..., description="Management level")
    is_decision_maker_role: bool = Field(..., description="Decision maker role")
    salary_min: float = Field(..., ge=0, description="Minimum salary")
    salary_max: float = Field(..., ge=0, description="Maximum salary")
    salary_currency: str = Field(..., description="Salary currency")
    salary_type: str = Field(..., description="Salary type")
    employment_type: str = Field(..., description="Employment type")
    remote_allowed: bool = Field(..., description="Remote work allowed")
    shift_schedule: str = Field(..., description="Shift schedule")
    skills: List[str] = Field(default=[], description="Required skills")
    posted_date: str = Field(..., description="Posted date (YYYY-MM-DD)")
    recruiter_name: str = Field(..., description="Recruiter name")
    recruiter_title: str = Field(..., description="Recruiter title")
    status: str = Field(..., description="Job status")
    
    @field_validator('posted_date')
    @classmethod
    def validate_date(cls, v: str) -> str:
        if not re.match(r'^\d{4}-\d{2}-\d{2}$', v):
            raise ValueError('Date must be in YYYY-MM-DD format')
        year, month, day = map(int, v.split('-'))
        if month < 1 or month > 12:
            raise ValueError('Invalid month')
        if day < 1 or day > 31:
            raise ValueError('Invalid day')
        return v

class CoresignalJobsScenario(BaseModel):
    """Main scenario model for Coresignal job market data."""
    jobs: Dict[str, JobDetail] = Field(default={}, description="All job postings by job_id")
    company_metadata: Dict[str, Dict[str, str]] = Field(default={}, description="Company metadata by company name")

Scenario_Schema = [Job, JobDetail, CoresignalJobsScenario]

# Section 2: Class
class CoresignalJobsAPI:
    def __init__(self):
        """Initialize Coresignal Jobs API with empty state."""
        self.jobs: Dict[str, JobDetail] = {}
        self.company_metadata: Dict[str, Dict[str, str]] = {}

    def load_scenario(self, scenario: dict) -> None:
        """Load scenario data into the API instance."""
        model = CoresignalJobsScenario(**scenario)
        self.jobs = {job_id: JobDetail(**job_data) if isinstance(job_data, dict) else job_data 
                     for job_id, job_data in model.jobs.items()}
        self.company_metadata = model.company_metadata

    def save_scenario(self) -> dict:
        """Save current state as scenario dictionary."""
        return {
            "jobs": {job_id: job.model_dump() for job_id, job in self.jobs.items()},
            "company_metadata": self.company_metadata
        }

    def search_jobs(self, keywords: str, location: Optional[str], posted_after: Optional[str]) -> dict:
        """Execute keyword-based job search."""
        results = []
        for job in self.jobs.values():
            if keywords.lower() not in job.title.lower():
                continue
            if location and location.lower() not in job.location.lower():
                continue
            if posted_after and job.posted_date < posted_after:
                continue
            results.append({
                "job_id": job.job_id,
                "title": job.title,
                "company": job.company,
                "location": job.location,
                "department": job.department,
                "seniority": job.seniority,
                "employment_type": job.employment_type,
                "remote_allowed": job.remote_allowed,
                "posted_date": job.posted_date,
                "status": job.status
            })
        return {"jobs": results, "total_count": len(results)}

    def get_job_details(self, job_id: str) -> dict:
        """Retrieve comprehensive job details."""
        job = self.jobs[job_id]
        return job.model_dump()

    def search_by_company(self, company_name: str, active_only: bool) -> dict:
        """Retrieve all job postings for a specific company."""
        company_jobs = []
        company_info = self.company_metadata.get(company_name, {})
        
        for job in self.jobs.values():
            if job.company.lower() != company_name.lower():
                continue
            if active_only and job.status.lower() != "active":
                continue
            company_jobs.append({
                "job_id": job.job_id,
                "title": job.title,
                "department": job.department,
                "location": job.location,
                "posted_date": job.posted_date,
                "status": job.status
            })
        
        return {
            "company_name": company_name,
            "company_hq": company_info.get("hq", ""),
            "company_industry": company_info.get("industry", ""),
            "jobs": company_jobs
        }

    def search_by_skills(self, skills: List[str], location: Optional[str]) -> dict:
        """Perform skill-based job search with relevance scoring."""
        results = []
        skills_lower = [s.lower() for s in skills]
        
        for job in self.jobs.values():
            if location and location.lower() not in job.location.lower():
                continue
            
            job_skills_lower = [s.lower() for s in job.skills]
            matched = sum(1 for s in skills_lower if s in job_skills_lower)
            
            if matched == 0:
                continue
            
            match_score = matched / len(skills_lower)
            required = [s for s in job.skills if s.lower() in skills_lower]
            preferred = [s for s in job.skills if s.lower() not in skills_lower]
            
            results.append({
                "job_id": job.job_id,
                "title": job.title,
                "company": job.company,
                "required_skills": required,
                "preferred_skills": preferred,
                "match_score": match_score
            })
        
        results.sort(key=lambda x: x["match_score"], reverse=True)
        return {"jobs": results}

    def get_hiring_trends(self, industry: Optional[str], location: Optional[str], time_range: Optional[str]) -> dict:
        """Analyze job market dynamics and hiring statistics."""
        filtered_jobs = []
        
        for job in self.jobs.values():
            if industry and job.company_industry.lower() != industry.lower():
                continue
            if location and location.lower() not in job.location.lower():
                continue
            filtered_jobs.append(job)
        
        company_counts: Dict[str, int] = {}
        title_counts: Dict[str, int] = {}
        
        for job in filtered_jobs:
            company_counts[job.company] = company_counts.get(job.company, 0) + 1
            title_counts[job.title] = title_counts.get(job.title, 0) + 1
        
        top_companies = [{"company": c, "open_positions": cnt} 
                        for c, cnt in sorted(company_counts.items(), key=lambda x: x[1], reverse=True)[:10]]
        top_titles = [{"title": t, "count": cnt} 
                     for t, cnt in sorted(title_counts.items(), key=lambda x: x[1], reverse=True)[:10]]
        
        return {
            "time_range": time_range or "30d",
            "total_postings": len(filtered_jobs),
            "new_postings": len([j for j in filtered_jobs if j.status.lower() == "active"]),
            "top_hiring_companies": top_companies,
            "top_job_titles": top_titles
        }

# Section 3: MCP Tools

    """
```
